body: Just spent 2 hours debugging a Rust lifetime issue. Turned out I needed to clone a string instead of borrowing.
The compiler error messages for lifetimes can be really cryptic. Did you try the NLL diagnostics?
Yeah the error pointed at the wrong line. The actual issue was three functions up in the call chain.
I've found that adding explicit lifetime annotations helps narrow down where the real problem is.
Good tip. I'll try that next time instead of just staring at the error output for an hour.
